description: Add GitHub pull request and issue templates that guide contributors toward awesome-list scope, one-resource-per-PR discipline, and maintainer review expectations—aligned with CONTRIBUTING.md, README.md, and docs/review-policy.md—without adding README list entries.

context.customerAsk: Add the missing GitHub pull request and issue templates that guide contributors toward the awesome-list scope and submission rules. Deliver .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md, .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/add-resource.yml, remove-resource.yml, broken-link.yml, and config.yml. Keep wording factual, concise, and consistent with one-resource-per-PR and non-promotional rules. Update related docs only when needed for consistency.
context.problem: Phases 1–5 established governance, review policy, automation, and GitHub Actions, but contributors still open unstructured issues and pull requests. Maintainers must repeatedly request missing metadata (resource name, URL, section, agent-factory relevance, format acknowledgements, link-health detail). Phase 6 checklist items for PR and issue templates remain unchecked.
context.solution: Add a PR template with submission metadata and self-check acknowledgements, three issue templates (add resource, remove resource, broken link), and config.yml that disables blank issues and routes contributors to CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/review-policy.md. Limit doc edits to consistency fixes; do not add README content in this batch.

acceptanceCriteria: .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md exists and aligns with CONTRIBUTING.md, README.md, and docs/review-policy.md
.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/add-resource.yml collects resource name, URL, README section, and why the resource fits agent-factory scope
.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/remove-resource.yml supports dead links, out-of-scope resources, archived projects, and misleading descriptions
.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/broken-link.yml collects enough detail for maintainers to verify broken or suspicious links
.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml disables blank issues and routes contributors toward contribution and review documentation
Template wording is factual, concise, non-promotional, and consistent with one-resource-per-PR discipline
Related docs (CONTRIBUTING.md, README.md) are updated only when needed to keep template guidance consistent with current reviewer policy
Quality gate: make check, make test, and git diff --check pass from repository root

userStories.title: Configure issue template chooser and contributor routing
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want GitHub to block blank issues and point me to the right contribution docs and issue types so I do not open unstructured reports.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml sets blank_issues_enabled to false
contact_links include at least one link to CONTRIBUTING.md and one to docs/review-policy.md with factual, concise about text
Config copy does not reference issue templates that are absent from the repository once the batch is complete
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add issue template for proposing a new resource
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want a structured issue form to propose a resource before opening a pull request so maintainers can confirm scope and section fit early.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/add-resource.yml defines a template for adding a resource with neutral, non-promotional description text
Required fields collect resource name (official title), canonical URL, README section, and why the resource fits agent-factory scope (groups of agents or flows)

README section is a dropdown with all ten README sections: Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, Protocols and Interfaces, Benchmarks, Research Papers, Blog Posts, Case Studies, Examples and Templates, Related Lists
Template body reminds contributors that pull requests must add one resource only and links to CONTRIBUTING.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add issue template for resource removal requests
userStories.description: As a contributor or maintainer, I want a structured issue form to request removal or relocation when an entry is dead, out of scope, archived, or misleading.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/remove-resource.yml defines a template for removal or relocation requests with factual description text
Required fields include affected resource name, README section or link text as listed, canonical URL, and reason for removal
Reason field covers at least dead link, out of scope, archived project, and misleading description, with optional detail textarea
Template references docs/review-policy.md removal guidance for relocate-vs-remove outcomes without duplicating the full policy
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add issue template for broken or suspicious link reports
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want to report broken or suspicious links with verifiable detail so maintainers can confirm link health and apply the broken-link outcome when appropriate.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/broken-link.yml defines a template for broken or suspicious link reports
Required fields include affected resource name (README link text), URL as listed in README, observed behavior (HTTP error, bad redirect, domain mismatch), and when the problem was observed
Template asks whether the reporter rechecked the URL and aligns with docs/review-policy.md link-stability guidance
Template points credible security concerns to SECURITY.md and avoids accusatory language
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add pull request template for resource submissions
userStories.description: As a contributor opening a resource pull request, I want a PR template that collects submission metadata and self-check acknowledgements so my change matches maintainer review expectations.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md exists with markdown sections for resource name, resource URL, target README section, and agent-factory relevance (why it belongs)

Template includes acknowledgement prompts for one resource only, scope fit, entry format (- [Resource Name](URL) - Description.), factual non-promotional description, description ends with a period, alphabetical placement, duplicate URL check, and live canonical link verified
Template links to CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/taxonomy.md, and docs/review-policy.md and reminds contributors to run make check (and make links when URLs change) before review
Wording is concise, encyclopedic, and non-promotional, consistent with CONTRIBUTING.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Align contribution docs with new templates (only if needed)
userStories.description: As a contributor reading CONTRIBUTING.md or README.md, I want any references to issue and PR intake to match the new templates so guidance is not contradictory.
acceptanceCriteria: If CONTRIBUTING.md or README.md mention opening issues or pull requests, only those sentences are updated to reference the new GitHub templates and chooser flow
If no doc changes are required for consistency, the implementing pull request states that explicitly with no gratuitous edits
No README resource entries are added or modified
No unrelated governance, workflow, or planner-owned file changes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify Phase 6 template delivery and quality gates
userStories.description: As a maintainer preparing Phase 6 convergence review, I want all five template artifacts present, internally consistent, and passing local quality gates so Phase 7 content work can start.
acceptanceCriteria: All required files exist: .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md, .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml, add-resource.yml, remove-resource.yml, and broken-link.yml
Issue template YAML files are valid GitHub issue forms with name, description, and body fields using recognized field types
Template copy states one-resource-per-PR in both the PR template and add-resource issue template and remains factual and non-promotional throughout
From repository root: make check, make test, and git diff --check exit 0
No README list entries, workflow changes, or bulk unrelated edits are introduced in this batch
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
